name: Above Ground Level
description: California-based boutique brand known for tree-themed molds and grippy, eco-conscious plastics. Popular molds include the Sycamore, Magnolia, and Baobab.

name: Axiom Discs
description: A vibrant, design-forward brand under the MVP umbrella featuring GYRO overmold technology. Known for the Envy, Hex, Crave, and Insanity.

name: Ching Sports
description: Recreational disc manufacturer known for early innovations like textured flight plates and beginner-friendly molds.

name: Clash Discs
description: Finnish manufacturer with premium plastics and food-themed molds. Known for the Berry, Mango, and Popcorn.

name: Dino Discs
description: Lightweight, kid-friendly discs produced through Gateway and ABC molds. Popular with families and new players.

name: Disc Golf Association
description: Founded by Ed Headrick, the father of disc golf. Produces classic molds like the Breaker, Hurricane, and Squall.

name: Discmania
description: Finnish brand with three production lines: Originals (Sweden), Evolution (Latitude 64), and Active (Yikun). Known for the P2, FD, and Cloud Breaker.

name: Discraft
description: Major US manufacturer and longtime PDGA partner. Home of the Buzzz, Zone, Nuke, and Paul McBeth line.

name: Divergent Discs
description: Manufacturer focused on beginner-friendly, affordable discs with durable plastics. Known for the Kraken, Leviathan, and Narwhal.

name: Dynamic Discs
description: Kansas-based Trilogy member known for the Judge, EMAC Truth, and Trespass.

name: Elevation Disc Golf
description: Specializes in ultra-flexible silicone discs designed to stick on landing. Known for the Interceptor and Binx.

name: EV-7
description: Putter-focused manufacturer founded by Phil Arthur. Known for the Penrose, Phi, and OG plastics.

name: Gateway Disc Sports
description: St. Louis manufacturer famous for the Wizard putter and wide range of flexible putter plastics.

name: Hyzer Bomb
description: Texas-based brand known for the Mortar and Tank, produced in partnership with Millennium.

name: Infinite Discs
description: Major retailer with its own disc line manufactured by Innova. Known for the Pharaoh, Sphinx, and Tomb.

name: Innova Champion Discs
description: The largest disc manufacturer in the world. Home of the Destroyer, Aviar, Teebird, and Firebird.

name: Kastaplast
description: Swedish manufacturer known for premium K1 plastics and cult classics like the Berg and Reko.

name: Latitude 64
description: Swedish Trilogy manufacturer producing high-quality discs for Latitude 64, Dynamic Discs, and Westside.

name: Lightning Golf Discs
description: One of the earliest disc golf manufacturers, known for lightweight, beginner-friendly molds.

name: Millennium Golf Discs
description: Co-founded by Innova and John Houck. Known for the Omega, Orion, and Polaris lines.

name: Mint Discs
description: Austin-based manufacturer known for consistent flight numbers and molds like the Alpha, Jackalope, and Lobster.

name: MVP Disc Sports
description: Michigan-based manufacturer and creator of GYRO overmold technology. Parent company of Axiom and Streamline.

name: Prodigy Disc
description: Founded by touring pros with a clean alphanumeric mold system (D, H, F, M, PA). Known for the PA-3, M4, and H3.

name: Prodiscus
description: Finland’s first disc golf manufacturer. Known for premium plastics and molds like the Jokeri.

name: RPM Discs
description: New Zealand manufacturer with molds inspired by native birds. Known for the Tui, Kea, and Piwakawaka.

name: Streamline Discs
description: Single-mold brand from MVP offering GYRO-inspired consistency without overmolds. Known for the Trace and Lift.

name: Thought Space Athletics
description: Oregon-based manufacturer known for premium artwork and molds like the Omen, Synapse, and Mana.

name: Trash Panda Disc Golf
description: Eco-focused manufacturer producing fully recycled and recyclable discs. Known for the Inner Core and Dune.

name: Westside Discs
description: Finnish Trilogy manufacturer known for the Harp, Maiden, and Sword.

name: Yikun Discs
description: China’s premier disc golf manufacturer and producer of Discmania Active Line. Known for the Tomahawk, Hu, and Kui.
